enonce.texte:
Complete the following sentences with the correct form of the passive voice.

Remember: Passive = be + past participle

questions.texte: The letter ________ (write) by my mother yesterday.
questions.reponse: The letter was written by my mother yesterday.

questions.texte: The house ________ (build) in 1990.
questions.reponse: The house was built in 1990.

questions.texte: The report ________ (finish) by the students right now.
questions.reponse: The report is being finished by the students right now.

questions.texte: The documents ________ (send) to the office tomorrow.
questions.reponse: The documents will be sent to the office tomorrow.

questions.texte: The cake ________ (eat) before we arrived.
questions.reponse: The cake had been eaten before we arrived.

enonce.texte:
Transform the following active sentences into passive sentences.

Identify the subject, verb, and object. Then, change the object into the subject of the passive sentence.

questions.texte: The teacher explains the lesson.
questions.reponse: The lesson is explained by the teacher.

questions.texte: The students will organize the event.
questions.reponse: The event will be organized by the students.

questions.texte: The government has built many schools.
questions.reponse: Many schools have been built by the government.

questions.texte: The company is producing new phones.
questions.reponse: New phones are being produced by the company.

questions.texte: The workers had completed the project.
questions.reponse: The project had been completed by the workers.

enonce.texte: Identify the tense used in each passive sentence and transform it into the active voice.

questions.texte: The book was read by millions of people.
questions.reponse:
Tense: Past Simple Passive
Active: Millions of people read the book.

questions.texte: The house is being painted by the workers.
questions.reponse:
Tense: Present Continuous Passive
Active: The workers are painting the house.

questions.texte: The decision will be made by the committee.
questions.reponse:
Tense: Future Simple Passive
Active: The committee will make the decision.

questions.texte: The problem has been solved by the engineers.
questions.reponse:
Tense: Present Perfect Passive
Active: The engineers have solved the problem.

questions.texte: The food had been eaten before we arrived.
questions.reponse:
Tense: Past Perfect Passive
Active: Someone had eaten the food before we arrived.

intitule: Using the Passive in Real-Life Contexts

enonce.texte:
Read the following news headlines and rewrite them in the passive voice.

1. The government has announced new education policies.

2. The local company will launch a new product next week.

3. The police are investigating the case.

4. The university awarded the scholarship to the best student.

5. The construction company built the bridge in record time.

questions.texte: Rewrite headline 1 in the passive voice.
questions.reponse: New education policies have been announced by the government.

questions.texte: Rewrite headline 2 in the passive voice.
questions.reponse: A new product will be launched by the local company next week.

questions.texte: Rewrite headline 3 in the passive voice.
questions.reponse: The case is being investigated by the police.

questions.texte: Rewrite headline 4 in the passive voice.
questions.reponse: The scholarship was awarded to the best student by the university.

questions.texte: Rewrite headline 5 in the passive voice.
questions.reponse: The bridge was built in record time by the construction company.

intitule: Identifying the Agent in Passive Sentences

enonce.texte: Identify the agent in each passive sentence. If the agent is not mentioned, explain why.

questions.texte: The cake was baked by my grandmother.
questions.reponse: Agent: my grandmother (mentioned because it is important to know who baked the cake).

questions.texte: The window was broken last night.
questions.reponse: Agent: not mentioned (unknown or unimportant who broke the window).

questions.texte: The letter was delivered by the postman.
questions.reponse: Agent: the postman (mentioned because it is relevant to know who delivered it).

questions.texte: The book was written in 1985.
questions.reponse: Agent: not mentioned (the focus is on when the book was written, not who wrote it).

questions.texte: The painting was created by a famous artist.
questions.reponse: Agent: a famous artist (mentioned because it adds important information about the painting).

intitule: Choosing the Correct Tense in Passive

enonce.texte: Choose the correct passive form in the context of each sentence.

questions.texte: The report ______ by the secretary yesterday.
questions.reponse: The report was typed by the secretary yesterday. (Past Simple Passive for a completed action)

questions.texte: The report ______ by the secretary right now.
questions.reponse: The report is being typed by the secretary right now. (Present Continuous Passive for an action in progress)

questions.texte: The report ______ by the secretary by the time you arrive.
questions.reponse: The report will have been typed by the secretary by the time you arrive. (Future Perfect Passive for an action completed before another future action)

questions.texte: The report ______ by the secretary by the time I arrived.
questions.reponse: The report had been typed by the secretary by the time I arrived. (Past Perfect Passive for an action completed before another past action)

questions.texte: The report ______ by the secretary. She finished it an hour ago.
questions.reponse: The report has been typed by the secretary. (Present Perfect Passive for a recent action with a result in the present)

enonce.texte:
You are a journalist writing a news report. Rewrite the following police report using the passive voice where appropriate.

Police Report: A man stole a car from the parking lot. The police arrested the man. They found the car in a nearby village. The police will return the car to the owner. The man will face charges in court.

questions.texte: Rewrite the police report using the passive voice.
questions.reponse: News Report: A car was stolen from the parking lot. The man was arrested by the police. The car was found in a nearby village. The car will be returned to the owner. Charges will be faced by the man in court.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why you chose to use the passive voice in this news report.
sousQuestions.reponse: The passive voice is appropriate in news reports because it allows the writer to focus on the events and the victims rather than the doer. It also creates a more formal and objective tone, which is suitable for journalism.

enonce.texte:
Read the following paragraph from a scientific article. Rewrite it using the passive voice where appropriate.

Original: Researchers conducted the experiment in a controlled environment. They measured the temperature every hour. They recorded the results in a database. They analyzed the data carefully. They published the findings in a scientific journal.

questions.texte: Rewrite the scientific paragraph using the passive voice.
questions.reponse: Rewritten: The experiment was conducted in a controlled environment. The temperature was measured every hour. The results were recorded in a database. The data were analyzed carefully. The findings were published in a scientific journal.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the advantages of using the passive voice in scientific writing.
sousQuestions.reponse: Using the passive voice in scientific writing allows researchers to emphasize the procedure and the results rather than the researchers themselves. It creates an objective and impersonal tone, which is a standard convention in academic writing. It also shifts the focus from 'who did it' to 'what was done'.

enonce.texte: Find and correct the errors in the following sentences.

questions.texte: The homework was finish by the students.
questions.reponse: Correction: The homework was finished by the students. (The past participle of 'finish' is 'finished'.)

questions.texte: The book is written by the author in 1990.
questions.reponse: Correction: The book was written by the author in 1990. (The action happened in the past, so we use Past Simple Passive.)

questions.texte: The car has being repaired by the mechanic.
questions.reponse: Correction: The car has been repaired by the mechanic. (The correct form is 'has been' + past participle for Present Perfect Passive.)

questions.texte: The letters were send to the wrong address.
questions.reponse: Correction: The letters were sent to the wrong address. (The past participle of 'send' is 'sent'.)

questions.texte: The movie will be show at 8 PM.
questions.reponse: Correction: The movie will be shown at 8 PM. (The past participle of 'show' is 'shown'.)

intitule: Creating a Story Using the Passive Voice

enonce.texte:
Write a short story (about 150 words) about a community event or a historical event in Cameroon.

Use at least five sentences in the passive voice.

Focus on the events themselves rather than the people who did them.

questions.texte: Write your story using the passive voice.
questions.reponse:
The Yaoundé International Book Fair

Every year, the Yaoundé International Book Fair is organized in the capital city. The event is attended by writers, publishers, and book lovers from all over Africa and beyond. The fair was founded in 1988 to promote African literature. Over the years, many important books have been launched at this event. Workshops and panel discussions are held during the fair. In 2023, a special award was given to a young Cameroonian writer. The event is supported by the Ministry of Culture and various international organizations. This fair is considered one of the most important literary events in Africa.

sousQuestions.texte: List the passive sentences you used in your story.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Every year, the Yaoundé International Book Fair is organized in the capital city. 2. The event is attended by writers, publishers, and book lovers from all over Africa and beyond. 3. The fair was founded in 1988 to promote African literature. 4. Over the years, many important books have been launched at this event. 5. Workshops and panel discussions are held during the fair. 6. In 2023, a special award was given to a young Cameroonian writer. 7. The event is supported by the Ministry of Culture and various international organizations. 8. This fair is considered one of the most important literary events in Africa.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why you chose to use the passive voice in this story.
sousQuestions.reponse: I used the passive voice to focus on the book fair and its activities rather than the organizers. This gives the text a more formal and objective tone, which is appropriate for describing a cultural event. It also helps to emphasize the importance of the event itself rather than the people behind it.
